State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5669              322-5901           322-6441              322-6444

June 9, 1981

Edward D. Young
Ayala Dinner Committee
P.O. Box 7177
San Bernardino, California 92411

Dear Mr. Young:

     This is to confirm the advice I provided to your office on
May 20, 1981, regarding the questions you asked in your letter
dated April 24, 1981.

     Your first question concerned the filing schedule for candi-
dates and committees.  The "Method for Calculating Threshold"
under Section B on page 5 of the "1981 Information Manual on
Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act" is
effective during 1981, a non-election year, for controlled
committees.

     Your interpretation of the "Method for Calculating Threshold"
is correct.  I would, however, like to add one clarification.
The second criterion, "...AND they contribute to, or make indepen-
dent expenditures on behalf of at least one other candidate or
measure..." include contributions to or expenditures on behalf of
all other political committees.  This interpretation is based on
Gov. Code Section 84200(b) which states:

     Notwithstanding the above, elected officers,
     candidates, and their controlled committees
     and committees formed primarily to support or
     oppose a candidate whose activities within a
     threshold period consist only of receiving
     contributions and making expenditures solely
     in connection with their own or their candi-
     date's campaigns are not required to file
     campaign statements pursuant to this subdivi-
     sion during any six-month period (from
     January 1 to June 30 or from July 1 to
     December 31) in which the candidate's office
     is not to be voted on ....

     Your second question is whether when a candidate's campaign
committee pays for the candidate's and the candidate's spouse's
flight, the spouse's flight is reportable as a gift.

Edward D. Young                    -2-                    June 9, 1981


     Assuming that the payment of air fare is in connection with
the candidate's campaign, i.e., the payment was political in
nature, rather than providing a personal benefit to the candidate,
neither the payment of the candidate's nor the spouse's air fare
would be a gift, and neither would be reportable on the candidate's
Statement of Economic Interests.

     If, on the other hand, the payment of air fare is of a purely
personal benefit to the candidate, both the payment of the candi-
date's and the spouse's air fare would be reportable on the
candidate's Statement of Economic Interests as a gift received
from the campaign committee.

     Please contact me if you have any further questions.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [illegible]

                                        Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Political Reform Consultant

RUBEN S AYALA
SENATOR, THIRTY-SECOND SENATORIAL DISTRICT

STATE SENATOR

California State Senate

April 24, 1981

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA    95804

Gentlemen:

Reference the State of California 1981 Information Manual on
Campaign Disclosure Provisions of The Political Reform Act, prepared by
the Fair Political Practices Commission. I am writing to obtain written
clarification of Page 5 of the Manual, "Method for Calculating Threshold:"

Question 1: Is this instruction for filing a Threshold statement
effective during non-election year, for a controlled
committee?

Is this interpreted that expenditure/contribution of $500
or more is only to be reported in Threshold statement if
the committee or candidate contributes to or makes
expenditures in behalf of another candidate or measure
during the Threshold period? If no expenditure is made
for another candidate or measure, it is my understanding
that even though breaking the $500 threshold for that
period, a report will not have to be filed; only annual
and semi-annual reports will be filed.

Question 2: Campaign committee pays for candidate's flight and spouse
accompanies candidate. Is spouse's flight reportable as
a gift?

Your reply to these questions will be greatly appreciated.

Sincerely,

EDWARD D. YOUNG, Treasurer
Ayala Dinner Committee #742 836

EDY/k

Reply to: Ayala Dinner Committee
P.O. Box 7177
San Bernardino, CA    92411

NOT PRINTED AT GOVERNMENT EXPENSE

LEGISLATIVE ADDRESS
STATE CAPITOL - ROOM 2048
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814
(916) 445-8994

ONTARIO, CA 91762

NATURAL RESOURCES AND

REVENUE AND TAXATION

STATE SENATOR
RUBEN S. AYALA
THIRTY-SECOND SENATORIAL DISTRICT

California State Senate

April 24, 1981

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA    95804

Gentlemen:

     Reference the State of California 1981 Information Manual on
Campaign Disclosure Provisions of The Political Reform Act, prepared by
the Fair Political Practices Commission. I am writing to obtain written
clarification of Page 5 of the Manual, "Method for Calculating Threshold:"

Question 1: Is this instruction for filing a Threshold statement
             effective during non-election year, for a controlled
             committee?

             Is this interpreted that expenditure/contribution of $500
             or more is only to be reported in Threshold statement if
             the committee or candidate contributes to or makes
             expenditures in behalf of another candidate or measure
             during the Threshold period? If no expenditure is made
             for another candidate or measure, it is my understanding
             that even though breaking the $500 threshold for that
             period, a report will not have to be filed; only annual
             and semi-annual reports will be filed.

Question 2: Campaign committee pays for candidate's flight and spouse
            accompanies candidate. Is spouse's flight reportable as
            a gift?

     Your reply to these questions will be greatly appreciated.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        EDWARD D. YOUNG, Treasurer
                                        Ayala Dinner Committee #742 836

EDY/k

Reply to: Ayala Dinner Committee
          P.O. Box 7177
          San Bernardino, CA    92411


NOT PRINTED AT GOVERNMENT EXPENSE

sections.question:
Is this instruction for filing a Threshold statement effective during non-election year, for a controlled committee?

Is this interpreted that expenditure/contribution of $500 or more is only to be reported in Threshold statement if the committee or candidate contributes to or makes expenditures in behalf of another candidate or measure during the Threshold period? If no expenditure is made for another candidate or measure, it is my understanding that even though breaking the $500 threshold for that period, a report will not have to be filed; only annual and semi-annual reports will be filed.

Question 2: Campaign committee pays for candidate's flight and spouse accompanies candidate. Is spouse's flight reportable as a gift?

Your reply to these questions will be greatly appreciated.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is this instruction for filing a Threshold statement effective during non-election year, for a controlled committee?

Is this interpreted that expenditure/contribution of $500 or more is only to be reported in Threshold statement if the committee or candidate contributes to or makes expenditures in behalf of another candidate or measure during the Threshold period? If no expenditure is made for another candidate or measure, it is my understanding that even though breaking the $500 threshold for that period, a report will not have to be filed; only annual and semi-annual reports will be filed.

Question 2: Campaign committee pays for candidate's flight and spouse accompanies candidate. Is spouse's flight reportable as a gift?

Your reply to these questions will be greatly appreciated.
